demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged care facility; bedbound and fully dependent for all care

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I'm the night nurse at her aged care home calling on her behalf. She's had a sore on her lower back near the tailbone for a couple of weeks, but since yesterday it's suddenly gotten much deeper and it smells really bad, and now she's in a lot of pain — she keeps saying her back is burning. The skin around it looks angry and red. Can a doctor help us tonight?
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The chronic sore over her tailbone has deteriorated fast in the last day — deeper, foul-smelling, with new severe pain that her regular paracetamol isn't touching, and we're worried about her overnight.

symptom_narrative.onset: the sore first appeared about two weeks ago as a red patch over the tailbone; it has suddenly deteriorated in the last 24 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: about two weeks, with a sharp worsening since yesterday
symptom_narrative.character: the nurses say it started as a red mark, then broke down; now it looks deep with black and yellow patches in it, thick discharge, and it smells foul; the skin around it is red, firm and warm
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she says it is burning constantly and it is the worst the site has ever hurt — a real change from before
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant burning pain, worse with any repositioning or dressing changes
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much; the regular paracetamol is no longer enough
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving her or touching the area
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: the room smells foul near the wound; she is more uncomfortable and unsettled than usual
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: she has had pressure areas before given how long she has been bedbound, but nothing like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely bedbound and fully dependent; cannot reposition herself at all

past_medical_history.how_managed: full nursing care in the facility

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing pressure area over the tailbone

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged care facility, fully dependent on staff for all repositioning and pressure care; currently nursed on a standard foam mattress

objective_data_offered.value: temperature 37.6 taken on the ward thermometer
objective_data_offered.timing: at the time of the call

objective_data_offered.value: blood pressure 110 over 70 and heart rate 95 on the facility obs machine
objective_data_offered.timing: at the time of the call

objective_data_offered.value: breathing rate 18 and oxygen saturation 97 percent on the ward monitor
on video the sore over her tailbone looks deep with black and yellow patches in the base and thick discharge; the skin around it appears red, firm and shiny
staff report a strong foul odour from the wound and that she is grimacing and lying on her side, distressed by the pain
her weight from the facility chart is about 55 kg
she is being nursed on a standard foam mattress with no pressure-relieving surface in place
